name.es: Kolach ucraniano de boda y Navidad
name.fr: Kolach ukrainien de mariage et de Noël

description.en: Ukraine's ceremonial ring bread: braided rings of soft egg dough nested and baked golden. Parents give it to newlyweds for prosperity, or stack it three-high with a candle for Christmas Eve, not to be confused with the small Czech kolache bun.
description.de: Ukrainisches Festtagsbrot in Kranzform: geflochtene Ringe aus weichem Eierteig, ineinander verschachtelt und goldbraun gebacken. Eltern schenken es dem Brautpaar für Wohlstand, oder es wird zu Weihnachten dreifach gestapelt mit einer Kerze in der Mitte.
description.es: El pan ceremonial ucraniano en forma de anillo: aros trenzados de masa suave con huevo, anidados y horneados hasta dorar. Los padres lo regalan a los novios como símbolo de prosperidad, o se apila en tres niveles con una vela para Nochebuena.
description.fr: Le pain cérémoniel ukrainien en forme d'anneau : des couronnes tressées de pâte briochée, imbriquées et dorées au four. Les parents l'offrent aux mariés pour la prospérité, ou l'empilent en trois étages avec une bougie pour la veille de Noël.

origin.region: Ukraine, with parallel Slovak, Rusyn and Carpatho-Rusyn variants across Central-Eastern Europe
origin.note: Close relatives include Slovak kolač and Carpatho-Rusyn wedding breads, all ring-shaped and ceremonial. Not to be confused with Czech/Texan kolache, a small filled sweet bun that is a completely different pastry.

ingredients.note: for egg wash, brushed before baking; not counted in hydration

yield.shape: one large braided ring (stacked 3-high for Christmas Eve)

keeping.note: Stays soft longer than a lean loaf thanks to the egg and butter; wrap well once cooled.

method.title: Build the sponge
method.summary: Whisk warm milk with part of the flour and the yeast into a loose batter, and let it stand until bubbly, about an hour.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Beat in the remaining flour, water, butter, honey, salt, eggs and flavorings until you have a smooth, elastic dough.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Cover and let the dough roughly double in a warm spot, about 1.5 hours.

method.title: Divide and shape
method.summary: Split the dough into a larger and a smaller piece; twist the larger piece into two thick ropes and braid the smaller piece from three thin ropes.

method.title: Form the rings
method.summary: Coil each braided length into a ring, nest the smaller ring inside the larger one on a baking sheet, and pinch the seams to seal.

method.title: Second rise and wash
method.summary: Proof until visibly puffy, then brush generously with egg wash.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Start in a hot oven to set the crust, then lower the temperature and bake through to a deep golden brown.

source.adaptation: Vanilla, rum and lemon extract combined into one flavoring line; egg-wash egg excluded from hydration % as a surface treatment, not dough liquid.
